title: One Piece
author: Eiichiro Oda
description: Monkey D. Luffy sets sail with a rubber body and an iron will, gathering a crew of misfits to find the legendary treasure and become King of the Pirates.

reviews.comment: The greatest adventure ever drawn. Oda's worldbuilding has no equal.
Started it on a whim, now I'm 1000 chapters deep. Worth every page.
Slow arcs here and there, but the payoffs are legendary.
Wano arc alone is worth the entire journey. Pure spectacle.
Made me laugh and cry within the same chapter. A treasure.
Huge cast can be hard to track, but the emotion always lands.

title: Berserk
author: Kentaro Miura
description: Branded for death, the lone swordsman Guts wages a brutal, blood-soaked war against demons and the fate that betrayed him.

reviews.comment: Miura's artwork is a once-in-a-century achievement. Devastating and beautiful.
Dark fantasy at its absolute peak. Nothing else comes close.

title: Death Note
author: Tsugumi Ohba
description: A gifted student finds a notebook that kills anyone whose name is written in it, igniting a deadly battle of wits with a faceless detective.

reviews.comment: The mind games between Light and L are unmatched. Couldn't stop reading.
Brilliant first half. The pacing dips later but still a classic.

title: Attack on Titan
author: Hajime Isayama
description: Behind towering walls, humanity's last survivors fight man-eating Titans and uncover a truth far darker than the giants themselves.

reviews.comment: Every twist recontextualizes the whole story. Masterful plotting.
Art rough early on but the storytelling more than makes up for it.
The basement reveal broke my brain. Genius foreshadowing.
Bleak, brilliant, and unforgettable. The ending divides people but I loved it.

title: Fullmetal Alchemist
author: Hiromu Arakawa
description: Two brothers pay a terrible price for forbidden alchemy and journey across the land to reclaim the bodies their hubris destroyed.

reviews.comment: Perfectly structured from first page to last. A flawless shonen.
Heart, action, and a villain you love to hate. Arakawa is a genius.

title: Vinland Saga
author: Makoto Yukimura
description: A young Viking driven by vengeance slowly learns there is more to life than war, in an epic of blood, honor, and redemption.

reviews.comment: The character growth from revenge to peace is profoundly moving.

title: Jujutsu Kaisen
author: Gege Akutami
description: To save his friends, a high-schooler swallows a cursed relic and is pulled into a hidden world of sorcerers and ravenous curses.

reviews.comment: The fights are inventive and the tension never lets up. Addictive.
Stylish and dark, though the lore gets dense fast.

title: Demon Slayer
author: Koyoharu Gotouge
description: After a demon slaughters his family and curses his sister, Tanjiro takes up the blade to hunt the demons and find a cure.

reviews.comment: Pure-hearted hero and gorgeous action. Easy to love.
Story is simple but the emotional beats really land.
The Mugen Train arc had me in tears. Stunning paneling.
Predictable at times, but the sibling bond carries it beautifully.

title: Monster
author: Naoki Urasawa
description: A surgeon's choice to save a boy's life unleashes a charming, remorseless killer, and a desperate chase across Europe to undo it.

reviews.comment: A masterclass in suspense. Every chapter tightens the screws.
Urasawa makes you feel the dread on every page. Brilliant.

title: Chainsaw Man
author: Tatsuki Fujimoto
description: Saddled with crushing debt, Denji merges with his chainsaw devil-dog to become a wild, monster-hunting weapon for hire.

reviews.comment: Chaotic, hilarious, and oddly heartfelt. Fujimoto is fearless.
Wild ride that refuses to play it safe. Loved it.
Part 1 is a perfect rollercoaster. The Katana Man arc is unreal.
Emotional gut-punches hidden under all the chaos. Brilliant.
Great art, but some arcs feel rushed. Still worth reading.
